title: cassia nicoyana
intro: Cassia nicoyana, also known as the Costa Rican senna or wild senna, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its laxative and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's seeds and pods contain anthraquinones, which contribute to its medicinal properties, and have been reported to provide health benefits such as relieving constipation, reducing fever, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Cassia nicoyana can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and has also been used in traditional medicine in various forms, including capsules, tablets, and topical creams.
